examples.title: Algorithm: Deadlock Detection for Multiple Instances
examples.description: Pseudocode implementing the deadlock detection algorithm for systems with multiple instances of resource types
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DeadlockDetection
INPUT: Available[1..m], Allocation[1..n][1..m], Request[1..n][1..m]
OUTPUT: Deadlocked processes (if any)

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Initialize
 Work[1..m] = Available[1..m] // Copy available resources
 Finish[1..n] = false for all i // Assume all processes are not finished
 
 // Step 2: Find a process that can complete
 WHILE true DO
 found = false
 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 IF Finish[i] = false AND Request[i] ≤ Work THEN
 // Process i can complete - simulate resource release
 Work = Work + Allocation[i]
 Finish[i] = true
 found = true
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF found = false THEN
 BREAK from loop
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Step 3: Check for deadlock
 deadlocked = []
 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 IF Finish[i] = false THEN
 Add i to deadlocked list
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Step 4: Return result
 IF deadlocked is empty THEN
 RETURN "No deadlock - all processes can complete"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Deadlock detected in processes: " + deadlocked
 END IF
END

// Algorithm: Process Termination Recovery
ALGORITHM TerminateProcessRecovery
INPUT: List of deadlocked processes
OUTPUT: Recovery action taken

BEGIN
 // Strategy: Terminate lowest priority process
 WHILE deadlock exists DO
 Select process P with lowest priority
 Terminate process P
 Release all resources held by P
 Run DeadlockDetection again
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the deadlock detection algorithm for systems with multiple instances of resource types. Step 1 initializes Work with Available resources. Step 2 iteratively finds processes whose requests can be satisfied with current available resources, simulating resource release by adding allocated resources back to Work. Step 3 identifies any unfinished processes as deadlocked. The recovery algorithm shows a simple termination-based approach that iteratively terminates processes until deadlock is resolved.

examples.title: Python: Deadlock Detection Implementation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of the deadlock detection algorithm with the example from the topic
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Deadlock Detection Algorithm Implementation
Based on the example from the topic with 4 processes and 3 resource types
"""

def detect_deadlock(available, allocation, request):
 """
 Detect if system is in a deadlocked state
 
 Args:
 available: List of available resources for each type [A, B, C]
 allocation: 2D list where allocation[i][j] is resources of type j held by process i
 request: 2D list where request[i][j] is resources of type j requested by process i
 
 Returns:
 Tuple of (is_deadlocked, finished_processes, deadlocked_processes)
 """
 num_processes = len(allocation)
 num_resources = len(available)
 
 # Step 1: Initialize Work = Available
 work = available.copy()
 
 # Mark all processes as unfinished
 finish = [False] * num_processes
 
 # Track execution order for demonstration
 execution_order = []
 
 # Step 2: Find processes that can complete
 changed = True
 while changed:
 changed = False
 for i in range(num_processes):
 if not finish[i]:
 # Check if Request[i] <= Work
 can_complete = True
 for j in range(num_resources):
 if request[i][j] > work[j]:
 can_complete = False
 break
 
 if can_complete:
 # Simulate: Work = Work + Allocation[i]
 for j in range(num_resources):
 work[j] += allocation[i][j]
 finish[i] = True
 execution_order.append(f"P{i}")
 changed = True
 
 # Step 3: Identify deadlocked processes
 deadlocked = [i for i in range(num_processes) if not finish[i]]
 
 return len(deadlocked) == 0, finish, deadlocked, execution_order


def print_matrix(matrix, title, row_labels):
 """Helper to print matrices nicely"""
 print(f"\n{title}")
 print(" A B C")
 for i, row in enumerate(matrix):
 print(f"P{i} {row[0]} {row[1]} {row[2]}")


def main():
 # Example from the topic
 # 4 processes (P0-P3), 3 resource types (A=7, B=2, C=6)
 
 print("=" * 60)
 print("DEADLOCK DETECTION ALGORITHM")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 # Current Allocation Matrix
 allocation = [
 [0, 1, 0], # P0
 [2, 0, 0], # P1
 [3, 0, 3], # P2
 [2, 1, 1] # P3
 ]
 
 # Request Matrix
 request = [
 [0, 0, 0], # P0
 [2, 0, 2], # P1
 [0, 0, 0], # P2
 [1, 0, 0] # P3
 ]
 
 # Available Resources
 available = [0, 0, 0]
 
 print("\nSystem Configuration:")
 print("Resource Types: A(7), B(2), C(6)")
 print(f"Total Resources: {sum(available)} available initially")
 
 print_matrix(allocation, "Allocation Matrix:", ["P0", "P1", "P2", "P3"])
 print_matrix(request, "Request Matrix:", ["P0", "P1", "P2", "P3"])
 print(f"\nAvailable Resources: {available}")
 
 # Run detection algorithm
 is_safe, finish, deadlocked, execution_order = detect_deadlock(
 available, allocation, request
 )
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("DETECTION ALGORITHM EXECUTION")
 print("=" * 60)
 print(f"Execution order: {' -> '.join(execution_order)}")
 
 print("\nFinal Status:")
 for i in range(len(finish)):
 status = "Finished" if finish[i] else "DEADLOCKED"
 print(f" P{i}: {status}")
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("RESULT")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 if is_safe:
 print("✓ NO DEADLOCK DETECTED")
 print(" All processes can complete successfully")
 else:
 print("✗ DEADLOCK DETECTED!")
 print(f" Deadlocked processes: {[f'P{p}' for p in deadlocked]}")


# Test with actual deadlock scenario
def test_deadlock_scenario():
 """Test with a system that actually has deadlock"""
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("TEST: Actual Deadlock Scenario")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 # Two processes, each holding one resource and waiting for another
 allocation_deadlock = [
 [1, 0], # P0 holds 1 unit of A
 [0, 1] # P1 holds 1 unit of B
 ]
 
 request_deadlock = [
 [0, 1], # P0 needs 1 unit of B
 [1, 0] # P1 needs 1 unit of A
 ]
 
 available_deadlock = [0, 0] # No available resources
 
 print("Allocation: P0[A=1,B=0], P1[A=0,B=1]")
 print("Request: P0[A=0,B=1], P1[A=1,B=0]")
 print("Available: [0, 0]")
 
 is_safe, finish, deadlocked, order = detect_deadlock(
 available_deadlock, allocation_deadlock, request_deadlock
 )
 
 if is_safe:
 print("Result: No deadlock")
 else:
 print(f"Result: DEADLOCK detected in processes P{deadlocked[0]} and P{deadlocked[1]}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
 main()
 test_deadlock_scenario()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the deadlock detection algorithm from the topic. It takes allocation and request matrices along with available resources, then iteratively checks if processes can complete. The algorithm simulates resource release by adding allocated resources back to the work vector. The main function reproduces the exact example from the topic with 4 processes and 3 resource types. A test function also demonstrates detection of an actual deadlock scenario where two processes hold resources the other needs.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m × n²) where m is resource types and n is processes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) for work, finish arrays

examples.title: C: Deadlock Detection Implementation
examples.description: C implementation of the deadlock detection algorithm for multiple resource instances
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides a complete deadlock detection system. It defines a SystemState structure to hold allocation, request, and available matrices. The detectDeadlock function implements the algorithm: it initializes Work with Available, iteratively finds processes whose requests can be satisfied, simulates resource release, and identifies any unfinished processes as deadlocked. The main function sets up the exact example from the topic with 4 processes and 3 resource types. The code also includes a recovery function that demonstrates process termination as a deadlock resolution strategy.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m × n²) where m is resource types and n is processes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m + n) for work and finish arrays
